stem: The relationship between two variables, x and y, is linear. For every increase in the value of x by 1, the value of y increases by 6. When the value of x is 3, the value of y is 25. Which equation represents the relationship between x and y?
rationale: Choice A is correct. A constant rate of increase of 6 in y per unit increase in x means the slope is 6. Using (3, 25): 25 = 6(3) + b → b = 7. So y = 6x + 7. Verify: at x = 3, y = 18 + 7 = 25 ✓. The other choices have either the wrong slope or wrong intercept (or both).
